item.description: President Trump is expected to make an announcement about plans to drop the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund. NBC News' Melanie Zanona has details on the backlash the fund has faced on Capitol Hill.

media:content.media:title: Trump to drop controversial 'anti-weaponization' fund
media:content.media:description: President Trump is expected to make an announcement about plans to drop the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund. NBC News' Melanie Zanona has details on the backlash the fund has faced on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Trump to drop controversial 'anti-weaponization' fund

item.description: President Donald Trump told NBC News’ Garrett Haake he had not been informed of Iran suspending peace talks with the U.S. which was reported by Iranian state media. NBC News’ Monica Alba reports on how the president reacted and if the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will be affected.

media:content.media:title: Iranian media: Tehran suspends U.S. peace talks over Israeli attacks in Lebanon
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump told NBC News’ Garrett Haake he had not been informed of Iran suspending peace talks with the U.S. which was reported by Iranian state media. NBC News’ Monica Alba reports on how the president reacted and if the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will be affected.

item.title: Iranian media: Tehran suspends U.S. peace talks over Israeli attacks in Lebanon

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley accused CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss of “murdering” the venerable newsmagazine at a staff meeting Monday with Nick Bilton, the technology journalist tapped to oversee the show, according to a source who was in the room and an audio recording

media:content.media:text: Scott Pelley
media:content.media:description: Scott Pelley, Correspondent, 60 Minutes on Sept. 25 2025.

item.title: Scott Pelley accuses CBS News’ Bari Weiss of ‘murdering’ ‘60 Minutes’

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Anthropic said it filed for an initial public stock offering, a surprise start in its race with OpenAI to be the next trillion-dollar AI startup to go public.

media:content.media:text: Anthropic PBC's Code With Claude Developer Conference
media:content.media:description: Anthropic has filed for an initial public stock offering, a surprise start to its race with OpenAI to go public.

item.title: Anthropic files for IPO before OpenAI as trillion-dollar startups race to go public

item.dc:creator: Abigail Brooks
Jared Perlo
item.description: Florida’s attorney general says the company misrepresents safety to turn a profit.

media:content.media:text: Open AI CEO Speaks At Annual Snowflake Summit In San Francisco
media:content.media:description: Open AI CEO Sam Altman speaks during Snowflake Summit 2025 at Moscone Center on June 02, 2025 in San Francisco, California. Snowflake Summit 2025 runs through June 5th.

media:content.media:text: Shooting At Florida State University Leaves 2 Dead And Multiple Injured
media:content.media:description: Community members gathered for a vigil in Tallahassee honoring the victims of the Florida State University shooting in April 2025.

item.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, accusing them of putting profit over safety

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
Angela Yang
item.description: Nearly 1 in 5 adolescents and young adults are turning to AI chatbots for advice when they’re sad, angry, nervous or stressed, according to a new study.

media:content.media:text: A close-up view of a person using a smartphone voice assistant feature
media:content.media:description: A survey asked people ages 12 to 21 whether they had used a service like ChatGPT or Google Gemini for mental health advice. Around 19% of respondents said yes.

item.title: Around 1 in 5 young people use AI chatbots for mental health advice, survey finds

item.dc:creator: Shannon Pettypiece
item.description: A Harvard study estimates that people who pay with cash and debit cards are subsidizing $30 billion a year in points and perks for credit card users.

media:content.media:text: Photo illustration of cash and credit cards with a diagonal arrow
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:text: Maurice Lamarche stands inside of a store
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:credit: Leila Register / NBC News; Illustrations by Ben Denzer
media:content.media:text: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Cody Newman holds a bag of unidentifiable snacks and a Gatorade bottle in the aisle of a store, left, and hands counting money at a registeer
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:text: An exterior view of Tiger Fuel gas station and market
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:text: Jayne Blair inserts a debit card into a card reader at a register inside of a store
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of a $100 bill be held over a counter by hands, left, and Calvin Buergey standing outside of his truck holding a bottle of soda outside
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

media:content.media:text: Hands on a counter counting change
media:content.media:description: Credit card fees raise prices for everyone, and the burden falls harder on customers who pay in cash, researchers said.

item.title: How shoppers who pay in cash are subsidizing Americans’ credit card reward points

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Joe Murphy
item.description: If you want to attend one of the opening matches of the upcoming World Cup, hotels and tickets are still widely available. But it’s going to cost you.

media:content.media:text: FBL-WC-2026-US-STADIUM
media:content.media:description: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J., ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: How much does it cost to go to a World Cup game? We did the math

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
Natasha Korecki
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro decried corruption in his primary night address to supporters in Bucks County last month.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump stands near U.S. flags inside of the East Room of the White House
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump in the East Room of the White House on May 22, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Gov. Josh Shapiro
media:content.media:description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro.

media:content.media:text: Brian Fitzpatrick 3/27/26
media:content.media:description: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Pa.

item.title: Democrats think their secret sauce in 2026 is targeting Trump and GOP on ‘corruption’

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
item.description: The World Health Organization’s leader traveled to the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo over the weekend, as suspected cases and deaths continue to mount.

media:content.media:text: Congo Ebola
media:content.media:description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus directorgeneral of the World Health Organization, visits health workers at the Evangelical Medical Center in Bunia, Congo, Sunday, May 31, 2026.

media:content.media:text: A health worker collects meals for Ebola patients at the treatment center in Rwampara, Congo
media:content.media:description: A health worker collects meals Friday for Ebola patients at the treatment center in Rwampara, Congo.

media:content.media:text: A man in protective equipment, raises awareness about Ebola at a market in Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo, on May 29, 2026.
media:content.media:description: A man in protective equipment raises awareness about Ebola at a market Friday in Goma, Congo.

item.title: Brazil and Italy rule out Ebola in previously suspected cases

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Jon Schuppe
item.description: New Mexico lawmakers said Monday they are demanding documents from an array of government and private institutions as the first major step in their effort to tell the full story of what Jeffrey Epstein did in the state — and whether any others should be prosecuted for crimes there

media:content.media:text: The mansion of Zorro Ranch in Stanley, NM.
media:content.media:description: The mansion of Zorro Ranch in Stanley, NM.

item.title: New Mexico lawmakers to blast out subpoenas in Epstein investigation

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: Other progressive members of Congress who endorsed Platner, the leading Democratic candidate for a U.S. Senate seat in Maine, are also coming to his defense.

media:content.media:text: Maine Democratic Candidate For Senate Graham Platner Campaigns Across The State
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner stand together during a “Fighting Oligarchy” tour stop at the Collins Center for the Arts on the University of Maine campus on May 24 in Orono, Maine.

item.title: Bernie Sanders defends Graham Platner after reports of sexually explicit text messages

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley accused CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss of “murdering” the venerable newsmagazine at a staff meeting Monday with Nick Bilton, the technology journalist tapped to oversee the show, according to a source who was in the room and an audio recording

media:content.media:text: Scott Pelley
media:content.media:description: Scott Pelley, Correspondent, 60 Minutes on Sept. 25 2025.

item.title: Scott Pelley accuses CBS News’ Bari Weiss of ‘murdering’ ‘60 Minutes’

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: Hurricane season in the Atlantic kicked off on Monday, and for the first time in more than a decade, forecasters expect it to be slower than usual.

media:content.media:text: Hurricane Melissa makes landfall as Jamaica's strongest-ever storm
media:content.media:description: A satellite view shows Hurricane Melissa over the Caribbean Sea, near New Hope, Jamaica, in 2025.

media:content.media:text: Hawaii Floods
media:content.media:description: A satellite view shows Hurricane Melissa over the Caribbean Sea, near New Hope, Jamaica, in 2025.

item.title: Hurricane season begins, with the first below-average forecast in a decade

item.dc:creator: Erika Edwards
Maggie Vespa
item.description: Five of the 18 U.S. cruise ship passengers quarantined in Nebraska went home Monday, halfway through their quarantine after being exposed to hantavirus, the CDC said.

media:content.media:text: Nebraska Medicine's Davis Global Center in Omaha, Neb. where American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship will quarantine.
media:content.media:description: American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have been quarantining at Nebraska Medicine's Davis Global Center in Omaha.

media:content.media:text: Jake Rosmarin shows off his quarantine room.
media:content.media:description: American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have been quarantining at Nebraska Medicine's Davis Global Center in Omaha.

item.title: State trooper guards and daily fever checks: Some hantavirus cruise passengers go home

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: Pregnant women, parents of young children, veterans with disabilities and several other groups will be exempt from Medicaid’s new work requirements, the Trump administration said Monday

media:content.media:credit: Astrid Riecken For The Washington Post via Getty Images file
media:content.media:text: People protest the upcoming Medicaid cuts
media:content.media:description: People protest Medicaid cuts in Washington, D.C., in May 2025.

item.title: Trump admin outlines who would be exempt from Medicaid work requirements

item.dc:creator: Phil Helsel
item.description: A teenage competitor is accused of injuring three horses with a knife at a barn near a Las Vegas competition over the weekend, police and horse show organizers said

media:content.media:text: Bay Horse Running in Barrel Race
media:content.media:description: Brown horse running barrel race.

item.title: Teen horse competitor accused of hurting animals during Las Vegas event

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Allan Smith and Natasha Korecki explore how Democrats are trying to turn the GOP’s “drain the swamp” message on its head.

media:content.media:text: Pennsylvania Residents Vote In Primary Election
media:content.media:description: Josh Shapiro, governor of Pennsylvania, during an election night event at The Fuge in Warminster Township, Pennsylvania, on May 19, 2026.

item.title: Democrats zero in on an anti-corruption message: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Kelly O'Donnell
Laura Strickler
Stacey Klein
item.description: More than 30 gallons of fuel spilled onto the National Mall from generators that were used during the recent Freedom 250 event.

media:content.media:text: Rededicate 250 Prayer Gathering On The National Mall
media:content.media:description: Attendees during Rededicate 250: A National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise and Thanksgiving on the National Mall on May 17.

item.title: 30 gallons of fuel spilled on National Mall after event for America’s 250th birthday

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Ryan J. Reilly
Frank Thorp V
item.description: The Trump administration signaled it is backing off on creating a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund announced by the Justice Department that could send money to allies of President Donald Trump.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Meets With His Cabinet At The White House
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump listens to members of his Cabinet speak during a meeting in the Cabinet Room of the White House on May 27, 2026 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Trump admin appears to back off $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after rare GOP backlash

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: 12-year-old New Jersey boy Xavier Taylor is in critical condition after being struck in the head during warmups before a baseball game last week.

media:content.media:text: Xavier Taylor stands on a baseball field in a Royals uniform and wearing a baseball glove on one hand
media:content.media:description: Xavier Taylor.

item.title: 12-year-old boy in critical condition after baseball strikes head during warmups

Natasha Korecki

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
item.description: Kash Patel’s girlfriend has filed a lawsuit against MS NOW, accusing it of using “sham” anonymous sources to “push knowingly or recklessly false allegations.”

media:content.media:text: Alexis Wilkins looks at her boyfriend, Kash Patel, as he speaks after being sworn in as the new Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigations (FBI) by U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi in the Eisenhower Executive Office Building at the White House on February 21, 2025 in Washington, D.C.
media:content.media:description: Alexis Wilkins looks on as her boyfriend, Kash Patel, is sworn in as the director of the FBI in 2025.

media:content.media:text: Acting AG Todd Blanche And FBI Director Kash Patel Hold Justice Department Press Conference
media:content.media:description: Alexis Wilkins looks on as her boyfriend, Kash Patel, is sworn in as the director of the FBI in 2025.

item.title: Kash Patel’s girlfriend sues MS NOW, accusing it of pushing ‘recklessly false allegations’

item.dc:creator: Tom Winter
Jonathan Dienst
David K. Li
item.description: Videos of men mysteriously emerging from New York City sewers have drawn the attention of police, who insist there’s “no threat to the public,” authorities said.

media:content.media:text: N.Y.C. Sewer Cover on City Pavement
media:content.media:description: A New York City sewer cover.

item.title: Police investigate videos of men mysteriously emerging from New York City sewers
